description: The rigorous foundation for integration and probability theory. Develops ŌÉ-algebras, measures, measurable functions, and the Lebesgue integral, resolving the limitations of the Riemann integral.

concepts.name: ŌÉ-Algebra
concepts.description: A collection ‚ĄĪ of subsets of X closed under: complement, countable union, countable intersection. (X,‚ĄĪ) is a measurable space. Borel ŌÉ-algebra ‚Ą¨(‚ĄĚ): generated by open sets. ŌÉ-algebras capture the notion of 'measurable' sets.
references: Rudin, Real and Complex Analysis, Ch.1; Royden, Real Analysis, Ch.1

concepts.name: Measure
concepts.description: A function őľ:‚ĄĪ‚Üí[0,‚ąě] satisfying: őľ(‚ąÖ)=0 and countable additivity (őľ(‚čÉE‚āô)=‚ąĎőľ(E‚āô) for pairwise disjoint E‚āô). (X,‚ĄĪ,őľ) is a measure space. A probability measure is a measure with őľ(X)=1.

concepts.name: Lebesgue Measure
concepts.description: Constructed via outer measure m*(E)=inf{‚ąĎ|I‚āô| : E‚äÜ‚čÉI‚āô} and Carath√©odory's criterion. Extends length to all measurable subsets of ‚ĄĚ. Translation invariant; m([a,b])=b‚ąía. Not all sets are measurable (Vitali set).
references: Rudin, Real and Complex Analysis, Ch.1; Royden, Real Analysis, Ch.2

concepts.name: Measure Zero
concepts.description: E has measure zero iff őľ(E)=0. Countable sets have measure zero; uncountable sets can (Cantor set). A property holds almost everywhere (a.e.) if the set where it fails has measure zero. The Cantor set: closed, uncountable, measure zero, nowhere dense.

concepts.name: Measurable Function
concepts.description: f:(X,‚ĄĪ)‚Üí(Y,ūĚíĘ) is measurable iff f‚ĀĽ¬Ļ(E)‚ąą‚ĄĪ for all E‚ąąūĚíĘ. For Borel: preimage of every open set is measurable. Every continuous function on ‚ĄĚ is Borel measurable. Measurability needed for Lebesgue integration.

concepts.name: Simple Function
concepts.description: ŌÜ = ‚ąĎaŠĶĘ1_{EŠĶĘ} (finite linear combination of indicator functions). ‚ąęŌÜ dőľ = ‚ąĎaŠĶĘőľ(EŠĶĘ). Every non-negative measurable function is the limit of an increasing sequence of simple functions (monotone approximation).

concepts.name: Lebesgue Integral
concepts.description: ‚ąęf dőľ = sup{‚ąęŌÜ dőľ : ŌÜ simple, 0‚Č§ŌÜ‚Č§f} for non-negative f; extend by f=f‚Āļ‚ąíf‚ĀĽ. Generalizes Riemann integral: every Riemann integrable function is Lebesgue integrable with same value. Integrates more functions; supports limit theorems.
references: Rudin, Real and Complex Analysis, Ch.1; Royden, Real Analysis, Ch.4

concepts.name: Convergence Theorems
concepts.description: MCT: if 0‚Č§f‚āô‚ÜĎf a.e., then ‚ąęf‚āô dőľ ‚Üí ‚ąęf dőľ. Fatou's Lemma: ‚ąęliminf f‚āô ‚Č§ liminf ‚ąęf‚āô. DCT: if |f‚āô|‚Č§g with ‚ąęg<‚ąě and f‚āô‚Üíf a.e., then ‚ąęf‚āô‚Üí‚ąęf. These allow interchange of limits and integrals under appropriate conditions.

concepts.name: LŠĶĖ Spaces
concepts.description: LŠĶĖ = {f measurable : ‚ąę|f|ŠĶĖ dőľ <‚ąě} with norm |f|_p=(‚ąę|f|ŠĶĖ)^{1/p}. For p=‚ąě: ess-sup. H√∂lder's inequality: |‚ąęfg|‚Č§|f|_p|g|_q for 1/p+1/q=1. Minkowski: triangle inequality. LŠĶĖ is complete (Riesz-Fischer).
references: Rudin, Real and Complex Analysis, Ch.3; Royden, Real Analysis, Ch.7

concepts.name: L¬≤ Space
concepts.description: L¬≤(őľ) with inner product ‚ü®f,g‚ü©=‚ąęfgŐĄ dőľ is a Hilbert space (complete inner product space). Orthonormal bases and Fourier series are central. Parseval's theorem: ‚ąĎ|‚ü®f,e‚āô‚ü©|¬≤ = |f|¬≤.
references: Rudin, Real and Complex Analysis, Ch.4; Stein & Shakarchi, Real Analysis, Ch.4

concepts.name: Product Measure and Fubini's Theorem
concepts.description: Product ŌÉ-algebra: ‚ĄĪ‚āĀ‚äó‚ĄĪ‚āā generated by rectangles A√óB. Product measure: őľ‚āĀ‚äóőľ‚āā(A√óB)=őľ‚āĀ(A)őľ‚āā(B). Fubini: if ‚ąę‚ąę|f|<‚ąě then iterated integrals equal the double integral. Tonelli: for non-negative f, order of integration may be switched freely.
references: Rudin, Real and Complex Analysis, Ch.8

concepts.name: Radon-Nikodym Theorem
concepts.description: If őĹ‚Č™őľ (őĹ absolutely continuous w.r.t. őľ), then ‚ąÉ measurable f‚Č•0 (Radon-Nikodym derivative dőĹ/dőľ) with őĹ(E)=‚ąę_E f dőľ. Generalizes density functions. Key tool in probability (conditional expectation) and functional analysis.

concepts.name: Abstract Measure Spaces
concepts.description: ŌÉ-finite: X = ‚čÉX‚āô with őľ(X‚āô)<‚ąě. Complete measure: if őľ(A)=0 and B‚äÜA then B measurable. Carath√©odory extension theorem: extend a premeasure from a ring to a ŌÉ-algebra. Lebesgue measure constructed this way from length of intervals.
references: Rudin, Real and Complex Analysis, Ch.1; Halmos, Measure Theory
